summary: A Go-based test runner and schema validation tool with MCP integration and Goss migration capabilities, heavily utilizing automated agent-generated tests.
concerns: High volume of auto-generated extra tests (GOrchestra/glm-agents) may bloat the codebase and reduce signal-to-noise ratio
Deep nesting of generated test directories suggests complex CI/orchestration overhead
Potential duplication between generated tests (e.g., mcp_extra_test.go) and mainline tests
suggestions: Consolidate or archive completed GOrchestra agent test directories to maintain repository cleanliness
Ensure generated tests are gated in CI to prevent runaway test execution times
Focus core development on the MCP handlers and Goss translator as they represent the primary business logic
Add clear documentation separating human-maintained tests from auto-generated ones
